metadata_details.purpose: Parser for Jamf Protect logs using the OCSF mapping to normalise events into the AI-SIEM schema.
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of the original Jamf Protect security logs.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – this parser processes endpoint security control events.
metadata_details.tags: jamf, protect, endpoint, security, control, macos, ocsf, parser

analysis.semantic_summary: Parser for Jamf Protect security logs that processes endpoint security events from macOS systems. It normalizes key security event attributes including user actions, process information, file hashes, and security verdicts into OCSF-compliant fields for standardized security monitoring and analysis.
optimization_opportunities: Add data validation for critical fields
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: jamf_protect-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Security Finding (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:27:52.891777

-- Pre-declare locals for performance
local type = type
local pairs = pairs
local os_time = os.time
local string_format = string.format

-- Validation helper functions
local function validate_string(value)
    return type(value) == "string" and #value > 0
end

local function validate_number(value) 
    return type(value) == "number"
end

-- Initialize nested table helper
local function init_nested_table(parent, key)
    if not parent[key] then
        parent[key] = {}
    end
    return parent[key]
end

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output structure with local reference
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "Security Finding", 
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "Security Control",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Jamf Protect",
                vendor = "Jamf"
            }
        }
    }

    -- Initialize nested objects
    local finding = init_nested_table(output, "finding")
    local actor = init_nested_table(output, "actor")
    local device = init_nested_table(output, "device")
    local process = init_nested_table(output, "process")
    local file = init_nested_table(output, "file")
    
    -- Optimized field transformations using local references
    if validate_string(record.eventType) then
        finding.title = record.eventType
    end

    if validate_string(record.user) then
        init_nested_table(actor, "user").name = record.user
    end

    if validate_string(record.computerId) then
        device.uid = record.computerId
    end

    if validate_string(record.processName) then
        init_nested_table(process, "file").path = record.processName
    end

    if validate_string(record.sha256) then
        init_nested_table(file, "hashes").sha256 = record.sha256
    end

    -- Copy message if present
    if validate_string(record.message) then
        output.message = record.message
    end

    -- Copy verdict/disposition if present
    if validate_string(record.verdict) then
        output.disposition = record.verdict
    end

    -- Validation and enrichment
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os_time() * 1000
    end

    -- Validate required OCSF fields
    if not validate_number(output.class_uid) or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid class_uid"
    end

    -- Add severity if not present (default to MEDIUM)
    if not output.severity then
        output.severity = "MEDIUM"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error handling wrapper
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

test_cases.expected: Full OCSF output with all fields mapped
